options.why: Wrote the numerator and denominator as digits; you must divide $3\div8=0.375$.
Stopped after one step; $3\div8$ continues to $0.375$.

questions.question_text: Write $0.35$ as a fraction in simplest form.

options.why: Correct place value, but not simplified; divide top and bottom by $5$ to get $\frac{7}{20}$.
Used only one zero; $0.35$ has two decimal places, so the denominator is $100$.
Turned the digits into a unit fraction; $0.35=\frac{35}{100}=\frac{7}{20}$.
Wrote the digits $3$ and $5$; you must compute $\frac{3}{5}\times100\%=60\%$.
Wrote the decimal $0.6$ with a $\%$ sign; multiplying by $100$ gives $60\%$.
Placed a point between the digits; $\frac{3}{5}\times100\%=60\%$.

questions.question_text: Write $24\%$ as a fraction in simplest form.

options.why: Correct start, but not simplified; divide top and bottom by $4$ to get $\frac{6}{25}$.
A percentage is out of $100$, not $10$: $24\%=\frac{24}{100}$.
Made a unit fraction from the number; $24\%=\frac{24}{100}=\frac{6}{25}$.
Moved the point three places; multiplying by $100$ moves it two places to $7\%$.
Moved the point only one place; multiplying by $100$ moves it two places to $7\%$.
Rounded off the repeating part; $\frac{1}{6}=0.1\dot{6}=16.\dot{6}\%$, which recurs.
Moved the point one place; $\frac{1}{6}=0.1\dot{6}$, and $\times100$ gives $16.\dot{6}\%$.
Used the denominator as the percentage; $\frac{1}{6}\times100\%=16.\dot{6}\%$.
